cards.text: {{c1::Header files}} provide a way to share {{c2::declarations}} across multiple {{c3::source files}} without {{c4::duplicating code}}.
Header files typically use the extension {{c1::.h}} or {{c2::.hpp}}. Implementation files use {{c3::.cpp}}.

cards.back:
Headers should contain:
  • Function declarations/prototypes
  • Class definitions
  • Const variable declarations
  • Type definitions
Generally declarations, not definitions.

cards.front: What should NOT go in header files?
cards.back:
Avoid in headers:
  • Function definitions (except inline/template)
  • Variable definitions
  • using namespace directives
  • Non-const global variable declarations

cards.text: Each source file (.cpp) should #include its corresponding {{c1::header file}} (.h) to ensure declarations match definitions.

cards.question: What should go in math.h vs math.cpp?
cards.answer:
math.h: int add(int, int);
math.cpp: #include "math.h"
    int add(int a, int b) { return a+b; }
cards.explanation: The header has the declaration, the .cpp file includes the header and provides the definition.

cards.text: #include {{c1::<header>}} searches {{c2::system/standard library}} directories. #include {{c3::"header"}} searches {{c4::current directory}} first, then system directories.

cards.front: Why should you #include as few headers as possible?
cards.back:
Including unnecessary headers:
  • Increases compilation time
  • May cause naming conflicts
  • Creates unnecessary dependencies
  • Makes code less maintainable

cards.explanation:
Follow these guidelines:
  • Every header should have header guards
  • Only include what you need
  • Header should be self-contained (include its dependencies)
  • Don't put 'using namespace' in headers
  • Match .h and .cpp file names
  • Order includes: own header, C++ standard, other libraries

cards.front: What happens when you #include a header file?
cards.back: The preprocessor literally copies and pastes the entire contents of the header file at the location of the #include directive.

cards.text: Never #include {{c1::.cpp}} files. Only include {{c2::header files}} (.h/.hpp). .cpp files are {{c3::compiled separately}} and {{c4::linked together}}.
